metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_1044
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Latrophilin-3 regulates the strength of synaptic input from L2/3 to L5 as shown by knockdown of latrophilin-3 in L2/3 neurons and electrophysiological measurements in acute slices (Fig. 1). Specifically, latrophilin-3 regulates strength of synaptic input, not by changing release probability (Fig. 2) but by controlling the density of synapses formed by L2/3 axons in L5 (synapse density in L5 was reduced when latrophilin3 was lost in L2/3 neurons; Fig. 3). The latrophilin-3 Olfactomedin domain was required for binding to FLRT3 (Fig. 5), and mutant latrophilin3 lacking the Olfactomedin domain was not capable of rescuing the deficit in presynaptic density following knockdown of endogenous latrophilin-3 (Fig. 6). Experimental description: All experiments involving mice in this study were carried out in accordance with protocols approved by the IACUC at the University of California San Diego and were performed in accordance with institutional and federal guidelines.

Embryonic day 16 (E16) mouse embryos from timed pregnant CD1 mice (Charles River) were injected intraventricularly with DNA
